criteria.description: All changes are confined to tests/. The ONLY permitted non-.py addition anywhere in the repo is tests/fixtures/sample.pdf, which must be a real, parseable, git-committed PDF (not a stub, not generated at test time) that documents.py's OWN extraction entrypoint can actually read. Any other scaffolding is limited to tests/__init__.py and/or tests/conftest.py. NO .py file outside tests/ is created, modified, or deleted. MODULE-BOUNDARY GUARD: the test suite imports ONLY documents.py plus stdlib and the extraction dependencies documents.py itself already uses; NO test (and no conftest.py) imports bot.py, wiki.py, or any Pipecat/STT/TTS/pipeline module, directly or transitively; and NO test imports or calls a PDF/text extraction library DIRECTLY — all extraction (including PDF) must be exercised through documents.py's own extraction entrypoint so pinned literals reflect production's extractor exactly. No new extraction library not already used by documents.py may be introduced by the tests. conftest.py (if present) contains only fixtures and pytest discovery config — no sys.path manipulation that shadows or shims production modules, and no autouse patches that alter documents.py behavior.
criteria.verifyBy: `git status --porcelain` shows changes ONLY under tests/; iterate every tracked/untracked .py path outside tests/ and confirm `git diff HEAD -- <path>` is empty for all of them; the only non-.py added file is tests/fixtures/sample.pdf; `file tests/fixtures/sample.pdf` reports a PDF, `git ls-files tests/fixtures/sample.pdf` confirms it is committed, and a test extracts non-empty text from it by calling documents.py's own extraction entrypoint (grep confirms no direct import/call of a PDF library in any test). grep/inspection of every test file and conftest.py confirms zero imports of bot, wiki, pipecat, STT/TTS/pipeline modules, or any extraction library not already used by documents.py; `pytest --collect-only -q tests/` succeeds without importing any Pipecat module (verified by inspecting collected module imports / no Pipecat in sys.modules after collection); conftest.py contains no sys.path shadowing or autouse behavior-altering patches.

criteria.description: Tests redirect the documents directory used by documents.py to a per-test pytest tmp_path, proven to work: a document written through documents.py's own API lands under tmp_path, and the real production documents directory is provably untouched via a recursive content-hash/listing snapshot (not just mtime) captured pre- and post-run. The monkeypatch targets whatever documents.py actually uses to resolve the directory (module constant, attribute, env var, or function) — the test must patch the real resolution path, not a dead constant. Every monkeypatch uses pytest's monkeypatch fixture (or equivalent) with automatic teardown so documents.py module-level state is restored after each test.
criteria.verifyBy: A fixture snapshots the real documents dir as a recursive {relpath: sha256} map before the run and asserts it is byte-for-byte identical afterward; a positive test writes via the documents.py API and asserts the file materializes under tmp_path (redirection proven, not assumed); running `pytest -q tests/` twice yields identical results with zero artifacts left in the real documents dir and git clean outside tests/; inspection confirms all patches use monkeypatch-with-teardown (no permanent module mutation).

criteria.description: Title-derivation is characterized against the ACTUAL function imported from documents.py (import the real symbol; no reimplementation). Every distinct output-affecting path present in the current title-derivation code is enumerated in a test comment/docstring referencing the source, and the enumeration count must be independently cross-checked against the actual documents.py source (the number of distinct paths in the comment must equal the number found by reading the function). A legitimate count of 1 is acceptable if the function is a single linear transform — the guard must NOT force fabricating branches. Each path is exercised with an exact-string assertion of the current output (extension stripping, separator handling, casing, whitespace/edge/empty handling as they currently behave). No path skipped.
criteria.verifyBy: Test imports the real function `from documents import <fn>` and asserts verbatim return values; the evaluator reads the real title-derivation code in documents.py and confirms the enumeration comment's path count matches the source (count of 1 accepted if that is the truth; no fabricated branches); each enumerated path maps to an assertion; `pytest -q tests/` exits 0 and the named title test exists and calls the real symbol; no assertion encodes desired (vs observed) output.

criteria.description: Upload-validation error paths are characterized with a 1:1 mapping between each rejection branch present in documents.py and a dedicated test, where the branch set is cross-checked against the actual source (unsupported/invalid type, oversized, empty, and any others actually present — count verified by reading documents.py, not from the generator's comment alone). For each branch, the test pins to the code's ACTUAL failure mode: if it raises, use `str(exc.value) == <verbatim message>` OR pytest.raises(match=re.escape(<verbatim message>)) plus an exact type assert of the MOST-DERIVED concrete exception type the code actually raises; asserting against catch-all Exception/BaseException is forbidden unless that is literally the exact type raised. If it returns an error value, assert exact equality. Raise-vs-return must match the real code. INTERPOLATED-MESSAGE RULE: where a branch's message contains interpolated runtime values (filename, byte-count, path, type name, etc.), the test MUST construct the expected message from the same test-controlled inputs it passed in (e.g. via the same format template) so the full message is asserted verbatim — `.startswith()`/substring/partial matching and copying opaque absolute paths or environment-specific values are forbidden. TARGETED-BRANCH RULE: the verbatim message/type asserted must be the one UNIQUE to the targeted branch, so an input that trips an earlier rejection cannot make the test pass for the wrong branch.
criteria.verifyBy: The evaluator reads documents.py's validation entrypoint, counts rejection branches, and confirms one test per branch (no under-enumeration); each test uses literal matching via `str(exc.value) ==` or `re.escape(...)` (never a raw regex-metacharacter message in match=) with the exact most-derived exception type asserted (no bare Exception/BaseException unless literally raised), or exact-equality error-value asserts per the code's real behavior; for messages with interpolated values the test constructs the expected string from its own controlled inputs (grep confirms no `.startswith`/substring/opaque-path copying); each asserted message/type is confirmed unique to its branch so the intended branch provably fired; `pytest -q tests/` exits 0; no source branch is left without a test.

criteria.description: Text extraction is characterized for EVERY format the current documents.py supports, with the supported-format set derived from and cross-checked against the actual extension→extractor dispatch/mapping in source (no under-declaring). One exact-output test per supported format calls the real extraction entrypoint THROUGH documents.py's own dispatch (no direct call to any extraction library), invoked with the REAL input type/signature that production callers use (path, file object, raw bytes, UploadFile, etc. — cross-checked against source), PLUS a test for the unsupported-format/fallback branch of the dispatch. ALL format tests (including PDF) assert the current extracted output with full `==` equality against a HARDCODED LITERAL in the test — substring/`in`/regex/partial assertions are forbidden for every format test. No normalization (.strip()/.replace()/casefold/whitespace-collapse) may be applied to hide current output; the literal must equal the extractor's actual present-day return value verbatim. The PDF path is exercised via committed tests/fixtures/sample.pdf through documents.py's own entrypoint; the PDF literal must be exactly what the repo's own extractor returns in the verifier's environment (regenerated by the verifier by running the entrypoint, not hand-guessed), and the fixture text must be chosen simple/stable enough (e.g. plain single-line ASCII) that the pinned literal is not extractor-version-fragile — OR the criterion explicitly declares the PDF literal environment-specific and the verifier regenerates it. The PDF test asserts the observed literal exactly with no post-processing.
criteria.verifyBy: The evaluator reads documents.py's extension→extractor mapping and confirms the test enumeration covers every entry plus the fallback branch; each format has a test asserting verbatim current extracted output via the real dispatch entrypoint using full `==` (grep confirms no `in`/substring/regex/normalization in any format test and no direct extraction-library call); the entrypoint is called with the real input type used by production callers (cross-checked against source); the PDF test loads tests/fixtures/sample.pdf, extracts via documents.py's own entrypoint, and asserts an exact hardcoded string literal that the verifier confirms equals the entrypoint's actual output in-environment with no post-processing; `pytest -q tests/` exits 0 and every enumerated format + fallback has a passing test.

criteria.description: List and load functions are characterized against the real documents.py symbols. After populating the tmp_path documents dir (via the API or directly, matching how the code reads it), listing asserts the exact current shape and — where the implementation's order is deterministic — exact order; where order is nondeterministic (e.g. os.listdir), the test characterizes it honestly (assert as a set or sorted) with a comment. Loading asserts exact current contents AND metadata. Empty-directory and missing-document behaviors are asserted verbatim: exact return value, or exact most-derived exception type (no catch-all Exception/BaseException unless literally raised) plus literal message via `str(exc.value) ==` or re.escape. INTERPOLATED-MESSAGE RULE: where a list/load message contains interpolated runtime values (e.g. a missing filename or path), the test constructs the expected message from its own test-controlled inputs — no substring/`startswith`, no copying opaque absolute paths. The enumerated list/load cases (list-shape, empty-dir, missing-document) are distinct characterization cases counted toward the c8 floor.
criteria.verifyBy: Tests import real list/load functions; list output is asserted verbatim with deterministic-order handling documented in a comment (sorted/set if source order is nondeterministic); load output asserts exact contents+metadata; empty-dir and missing-document paths assert the exact current return value or exact most-derived exception type+literal message (no bare Exception, no raw-regex match=); any interpolated message is reconstructed from test-controlled inputs (grep confirms no substring/opaque-path copying); `pytest -q tests/` exits 0 and is stable across repeated runs.

criteria.description: The suite is deterministic, hermetic (no network, no machine-specific state), imports and calls the ACTUAL documents.py symbols throughout (no logic reimplemented in tests), and documents observed present-day behavior rather than correctness. All assertions pin current outputs/error types/messages verbatim. HERMETIC WRITE CONTAINMENT: no test writes to any filesystem location outside its pytest tmp_path; the repo working tree is git-clean outside tests/ after the run, the real documents dir snapshot is unchanged (c2), and per-test monkeypatch teardown restores documents.py module state so test ordering-independence is guaranteed, not incidental.
criteria.verifyBy: `pytest -q tests/` exits 0 on a clean checkout and again on immediate re-run with identical results and no leftover artifacts; running tests in a shuffled/reversed order yields identical results (ordering-independence); grep/inspection confirms tests import documents.py symbols (`from documents import ...`) rather than duplicating its logic; no test performs network I/O; no test writes outside tmp_path (verified by the c2 snapshot + git-clean-outside-tests check); every assertion reflects observed behavior only.

criteria.description: Collection/skip guard: the suite must actually RUN its characterization tests, not silently skip, xfail, or deselect hard branches. The number of tests that execute and pass must be at least the total count of enumerated title paths (c3) + validation rejection branches (c4) + supported formats + fallback branch (c5) + list/load enumerated cases (list-shape, empty-dir, missing-document) (c6), with zero skipped, xfailed, or deselected tests. Parametrized cases count individually toward this floor, AND each enumerated source branch/path/format must map to a DISTINCT test or parametrize id/assertion — no two source branches may be collapsed into a single case, and no parametrize id may cover more than one enumerated branch.
criteria.verifyBy: `pytest -rA -q tests/` reports N passed with 0 skipped, 0 xfailed, 0 xpassed, 0 deselected, 0 errors; N is greater than or equal to the sum of enumerated paths/branches/formats/list-load cases cross-checked against documents.py in c3/c4/c5/c6; the evaluator confirms each enumerated source branch maps to a distinct test/parametrize id (no two branches collapsed into one case); no `@pytest.mark.skip`/`xfail` decorators or conditional skips hide any enumerated case.

criteria.description: A new file `session_state.py` exists at the Voice Tutor project root containing ONLY: (i) the pure, Pipecat-free session-state helper functions extracted verbatim from `bot.py`; (ii) any Pipecat-free module-level bindings those helpers transitively require (see c2 — constants, classes, enums, dataclasses, and other pure helpers); and (iii) the Pipecat-free import statements those helpers/bindings require (copied — not deleted — from git-HEAD `bot.py`, since `bot.py` may still need them). The membership definition of 'pure, Pipecat-free session-state helper' is authoritatively specified in c2 (this criterion references, and does not redefine, it). `session_state.py` contains no content from `wiki.py`, no STT/TTS/Pipecat pipeline code, and no LLM-backed/recap functions. Module import is pure: no top-level executable side effects and no top-level import of pipecat/STT/TTS/pipeline/LLM/recap. `session_state.py` is usable WITHOUT the Pipecat stack: it can be imported and its helpers invoked in a process where `bot`/pipecat are never imported.
criteria.verifyBy: Confirm `session_state.py` exists. AST-scan its module body: assert every top-level statement is exactly one of {the moved helper FunctionDefs, the moved required bindings per c2, an import statement whose imported module/name is Pipecat/STT/TTS/pipeline/LLM/recap-free}; any other content fails. Confirm its import list contains none of the forbidden stack. In a subprocess, `import session_state` WITHOUT importing `bot` or pipecat; assert zero stdout/stderr side effects at import time; then invoke at least one moved helper on a representative input from the c8 corpus and confirm it runs without ImportError/NameError. Confirm `wiki.py` is byte-identical to git HEAD via `git diff --exit-code -- wiki.py`.

criteria.description: SCOPE ENUMERATION — completeness and exclusivity with an INDEPENDENTLY re-derivable ground truth. A committed human-readable manifest (a top-level list literal `MOVED_HELPERS` in the test module, or `tests/fixtures/moved_helpers.txt`) records the exact function names moved, derived from git-HEAD `bot.py`. This criterion is the SINGLE authoritative definition of 'pure, Pipecat-free session-state helper' (c1 references it). A module-level `def` in git-HEAD `bot.py` QUALIFIES iff, following the module-level name-reference graph, it does not reference — directly or transitively through any module-level binding defined in `bot.py` — any pipecat/STT/TTS/pipeline symbol, nor any LLM-backed or recap function. Rules: (a) filesystem/OS/network I/O is NOT itself disqualifying — a helper that only touches session persistence and no forbidden symbol still qualifies; (b) inter-helper calls among qualifying helpers are PERMITTED; (c) any module-level BINDING a qualifying helper references transitively — constant, class, enum, dataclass, or other pure helper — that is itself Pipecat/STT/TTS/pipeline/LLM/recap-free MUST also move to `session_state.py`; imported names (stdlib/third-party/project-local) that are Pipecat-free are satisfied by copying the corresponding import statement into `session_state.py` (c1.iii) rather than by moving a definition; (d) any def/binding transitively touching a forbidden symbol is DISQUALIFIED and stays in `bot.py`. The rule is applied to git-HEAD source, not the post-edit state. EVERY qualifying helper is moved (completeness); NO non-qualifying function is moved (exclusivity).
criteria.verifyBy: From git-HEAD `bot.py`, the verifier independently enumerates all module-level `def`s, builds the module-level name-reference graph including class/enum/dataclass bindings and imported names, and classifies each per (a)-(d), producing a candidate function set, a required-binding set, and a required-import set. Assert (1) the committed manifest equals the independently-derived candidate function set; (2) the set of functions defined in `session_state.py` equals the manifest exactly — no qualifying helper left as a `def` in `bot.py`, none non-qualifying present; (3) every required binding (constant/class/enum/dataclass/pure helper) from the closure is present in `session_state.py`; (4) every required import is present in `session_state.py`. Classification is reproducible from source, not a generator judgment call.

criteria.description: VERBATIM MOVE — enforced by BOTH source-span and AST equality. For each moved helper AND each moved required binding (constant/class/enum/dataclass/pure helper per c2c), the exact source text of its definition span (from the `def`/`class`/assignment or decorator line through the end of its body, including inline comments and blank lines within the span) in `session_state.py` is byte-identical to its git-HEAD span in `bot.py`; top-level defs must match with zero dedent (dedent-normalization of the outermost level permitted ONLY if the original was nested). Additionally, the parsed AST nodes (`FunctionDef`/`ClassDef`/`Assign`/`AnnAssign`) must be structurally equal. Renaming, signature/default changes, decorator/docstring edits, comment removal, body reflow, or any logic edit is sprint failure. Copied import statements (c1.iii) are exempt from span-move identity but must import the same names as their HEAD counterparts.
criteria.verifyBy: For each moved function/binding: (1) extract the source span from git-HEAD `bot.py` and from working `session_state.py`; assert byte-equality of the span (captures comments, which AST cannot). (2) Parse each span's single top-level node and assert `ast.dump(node, annotate_fields=True, include_attributes=False)` is identical (line/col offsets excluded). For copied imports, assert the imported name set in `session_state.py` is a superset-consistent copy of the corresponding HEAD import names. Any difference in any check fails the sprint.

criteria.description: `bot.py` re-imports the relocated helpers (and any moved bindings its remaining code still references) from `session_state.py` so its public surface is unchanged: every moved helper remains resolvable as `bot.<name>`, and all pre-existing internal call sites still reference the same unqualified names.
criteria.verifyBy: Import `bot`; assert `hasattr(bot, name)` for every helper in the c2 manifest. Confirm `bot.py` contains a `from session_state import (...)` statement importing exactly the moved names (helpers plus any moved bindings still referenced by remaining `bot.py` code), that the original in-body `def`s/binding-assignments for moved items are removed from `bot.py`, and that no call-site name changed (via git diff).

criteria.description: BOT.PY DIFF IS TOTAL and MECHANICAL. The complete difference of working-tree `bot.py` relative to HEAD consists of EXACTLY: (a) removal of the moved helpers' and moved bindings' definition spans; and (b) addition of ONE `from session_state import (...)` statement re-importing exactly the moved names (statement MAY span multiple physical lines / be parenthesized). Pipecat-free import statements copied into `session_state.py` (c1.iii) STAY in `bot.py` — they are copied, not moved — so they contribute NO diff to `bot.py`. EVERY other byte of `bot.py` is identical to HEAD. No edits to STT/TTS/Pipecat pipeline code, no edits to LLM-backed recap functions, no edits to any non-moved binding. Blank-line churn around removed spans is normalized by collapsing any run of ≥1 consecutive blank lines to the file's prevailing inter-top-level-def blank-line count (measured from HEAD `bot.py`), applied IDENTICALLY to both the reconstruction and the working tree, so PEP 8 double-blank spacing is not falsely rejected.
criteria.verifyBy: Reconstruct expected `bot.py` from HEAD by deleting exactly the moved spans (per c2/c3) and inserting the single `from session_state import (...)` statement at the import location. Compute the prevailing inter-def blank-line count from HEAD; apply the identical blank-line-run→prevailing-count normalization to BOTH the reconstruction and the working `bot.py`; assert byte-equality. This is a TOTAL check — any other changed byte fails. Independently, extract each pipeline function and each recap function body from HEAD and working `bot.py` and assert byte-identity as a redundant guard.

criteria.description: RE-EXPORT IDENTITY. For each helper in the manifest, `getattr(bot, name) is getattr(session_state, name)` — `bot` exposes the very same function object defined in `session_state.py`, not a copy or re-wrapped function.
criteria.verifyBy: A pytest test imports both modules and asserts `bot.<helper> is session_state.<helper>` for EVERY helper in the c2 manifest.

criteria.description: IMPORT SAFETY and FREE-VARIABLE resolution. Both `import session_state` and `import bot` succeed in the test environment. No moved helper has unresolved free/global names: every module-level binding a moved helper references (per c2's closure) was moved to `session_state.py`, and every imported name it needs is satisfied by a copied import in `session_state.py`; no moved helper silently depends on a `bot.py` global that stayed behind. Importing `bot` merely to access the moved helpers does not require the Pipecat/STT/TTS stack.
criteria.verifyBy: pytest imports both modules successfully. For each moved helper, statically compute its free/global names (via AST + code-object `co_names`) and assert each resolves within `session_state`'s namespace or its imports. Additionally invoke each moved helper (through `session_state`) on the c8 corpus inputs and confirm no `NameError`/`AttributeError` from missing globals.

criteria.description: SHARED INPUT CORPUS + NON-VACUOUS representative characterization. A single committed input corpus `CASES` (a dict mapping each manifest helper name to a list of concrete, non-trivial argument tuples, including representative and edge/error cases) lives under `tests/` and is the ONLY input source consumed by c8, c9, c10, and c11. Under `tests/`, for EVERY helper in the c2 manifest, at least one test asserts its exact present-day return value on a representative `CASES` input, pinning a concrete literal value or concrete data structure. Tautological/self-referential assertions (`f(x)==f(x)`), truthiness-only checks, and comparing a helper to itself are prohibited.
criteria.verifyBy: Confirm the committed `CASES` corpus exists, covers every manifest helper with ≥1 representative and ≥1 edge/error case each, and that c9/c10/c11 tests read their inputs from `CASES` (not inline literals). Run pytest; confirm a representative test exists per manifest helper asserting output equals a hard-coded literal/structure, not another invocation of the same helper and not merely `assert result`.

criteria.description: NON-VACUOUS branch/edge/error coverage, ENFORCED BY A COVERAGE INSTRUMENT over the shared corpus. For EVERY manifest helper, the characterization tests exercise each branch/return path REACHABLE through the helper's public contract via `CASES` inputs, asserting a concrete current output literal or using `pytest.raises` on the EXACT current exception TYPE. Branch coverage collected while running the characterization tests must report 100% of each moved helper's OWN reachable branches/lines; provably-unreachable defensive branches (e.g., dead guards not reachable through the public interface) may be excluded via `# pragma: no cover` with a one-line justification, so a correct verbatim move cannot be failed by dead code. Exception MESSAGE matching uses verbatim equality only when the message is a static literal; where the message contains environment-dependent content (paths, temp dirs, input repr, interpolated values) a stable substring/regex via `pytest.raises(..., match=...)` is required.
criteria.verifyBy: Run `coverage run --branch` (or pytest-cov branch=true) scoped to the moved helpers over the characterization tests; assert 100% branch+line coverage of every moved helper's own body EXCLUDING lines explicitly marked `# pragma: no cover`. For each such pragma, confirm a justification comment is present and that the branch is genuinely unreachable through the public contract (verifier spot-checks). For each covered branch confirm a concrete assertion (output literal, or exact exception type with literal-or-`match` message per the static/dynamic rule). Reject placeholder 'e.g.' tests.

criteria.description: DUAL-IMPORT INTERFACE PROOF over the shared corpus. For EVERY manifest helper, the tests invoke it through BOTH `bot.<helper>` (re-export) and `session_state.<helper>` on EVERY `CASES` input (representative and edge) and assert equal results (or identical raised exception type/message), proving the relocation preserved the interface across both import paths.
criteria.verifyBy: A pytest test iterates the full c2 manifest and, for every input in `CASES[helper]`, asserts `bot.<helper>(args) == session_state.<helper>(args)` (or identical exception behavior). Not a subset — every helper and every corpus case.

criteria.description: BEHAVIOR-EQUIVALENCE GOLDEN with a VERIFIER-REGENERATED baseline over the shared corpus. Golden outputs (return values, or captured exception type/message) for every manifest helper on every `CASES` input are produced by the verifier from git-HEAD source, and the post-move helper (via `session_state`) must reproduce them identically. Because HEAD `bot.py` may import the Pipecat stack at module top level and thus be unimportable standalone, the baseline is regenerated by extracting the HEAD source spans of the manifest helpers AND their required bindings/imports (per c2) and exec'ing them in an ISOLATED namespace — never by importing whole HEAD `bot.py`. Any committed golden fixture is advisory only and MUST byte-compare against the verifier-regenerated baseline; the pass/fail decision rests on the regenerated baseline.
criteria.verifyBy: The verifier extracts from git-HEAD `bot.py` the manifest helpers' spans plus their required binding/import spans, execs them in an isolated module namespace, and records outputs for each manifest helper on every `CASES` input — regenerating the golden without importing whole HEAD `bot.py`. Assert the post-move helper's outputs (via `session_state`) equal this regenerated baseline for every helper and every input. If a golden fixture is committed, assert it is byte-equal to the regenerated baseline. Any divergence fails; a fixture matching NEW behavior but not the regenerated HEAD baseline fails.

criteria.description: The full test suite is discoverable and passes from the project root with zero failures/errors, and the ONLY production changes are the `bot.py`→`session_state.py` relocation plus `bot.py`'s single re-import statement (c5). Permitted non-production additions: test files under `tests/` (including the `CASES` corpus and optional advisory golden fixture), minimal discovery scaffolding (`tests/__init__.py`/`conftest.py`) if required, and the manifest (c2). The prior sprint's `documents.py` characterization tests are protected WHOLESALE: they may not be deleted, skipped, xfailed, weakened, or have assertions modified; their node ids and count are preserved and they still pass. No new global markers, `filterwarnings`-as-errors changes, or conftest/pytest-config edits that would alter the prior tests' collection or outcome.
criteria.verifyBy: Run `pytest` from project root: zero failures/errors. Run `git diff --name-only HEAD`: confirm production changes are limited to new `session_state.py` and `bot.py`'s minimal diff (c5); `wiki.py`, pipeline, and recap code unchanged; all other touched files are test-only (tests/, conftest, manifest, corpus). Enumerate the prior `documents.py` test node ids from HEAD and assert every one is still collected and passing with unchanged assertions (no deletion/skip/xfail/weakening); assert no new global marker/filterwarnings/conftest change alters their outcome.
